title: rubus microphyllus subsp. koehneanus
intro: Rubus microphyllus subsp. koehneanus, also known as the Chinese bramble or Koehne's dwarf bramble, is a subspecies of the Rubus microphyllus plant, belonging to the rose family and native to the mountainous regions of China. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, primarily its anthocyanin-rich leaves and stems, which exhibit antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial activities. The health benefits of Rubus microphyllus subsp. koehneanus include reducing oxidative stress, improving cardiovascular health, and potentially alleviating symptoms of diabetes and certain types of cancer. In traditional Chinese medicine, the plant is used in herbal preparations, such as infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, to treat various ailments and promote overall well-being.
